checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent end=2011-01-02, value=46200000; OtherAssetsNoncurrent end=2013-06-30, value=104200000

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet end=2012-01-01, value=219400000; InventoryNet end=2012-12-30, value=281200000
SalesRevenueNet FY2011=1941900000; SalesRevenueNet FY2012=2127300000 (+9.6% growth) vs inventory growth of +28.2% over same period

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic Q2 2011 (2011-04-04 to 2011-07-03)=152300000, versus surrounding quarters Q1 2011=32000000, Q3 2011=34100000, Q4 2011=36800000

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end=2012-01-01, value=3800000; end=2012-12-30, value=4800000 (+26%), roughly tracking AR growth of +29.7% over same period
evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 Notes - Allowance for Doubtful Accounts

evidence.quote: StandardProductWarrantyAccrual end=2012-01-01, value=13300000; end=2013-06-30, value=19700000, growing broadly in line with revenue growth

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2013-02-26 (accession 0001094285-13-000064); 10-Q filed 2013-05-07 (accession 0001094285-13-000140); 10-Q filed 2013-08-02 (accession 0001094285-13-000180) - all filed on schedule with no NT filings or 10-K/10-Q amendments in the 2010-2013 window

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans quarterly and annual figures from FY2007/2008 through Q2 2013 across balance sheet, income statement and cash flow line items (e.g., SalesRevenueNet series from 2008-12-28 through 2013-06-30; AccountsReceivableNetCurrent from 2010-01-03 through 2013-06-30)

top_signals: Receivables grew ~29.7% in FY2012 (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent 270000000->350300000) versus revenue growth of only ~9.6% (SalesRevenueNet 1941900000->2127300000), though this coincides with major M&A activity (Goodwill 546300000->990200000) that could explain consolidation-driven balance sheet growth rather than organic mismatch
Inventory grew ~28.2% in FY2012 (InventoryNet 219400000->281200000) far outpacing 9.6% revenue growth, warranting scrutiny of potential channel stuffing, obsolescence risk, or program build timing

Goodwill and other non-current soft assets nearly doubled (Goodwill 546300000 at 2011-01-02 to 990200000 at 2012-12-30; OtherAssetsNoncurrent 46200000 to 104200000) versus ~29% cumulative revenue growth over the same period, consistent with an aggressive acquisition strategy that elevates future impairment and purchase-accounting risk

Two consecutive Q1 periods (2012 and 2013) show positive net income but negative operating cash flow (NetIncomeLoss 35700000/CFO -19700000 in Q1 2012; NetIncomeLoss 40400000/CFO -56700000 in Q1 2013), a seasonal pattern that nonetheless merits monitoring for working-capital or accrual quality issues

An unusually large one-time net income spike in Q2 2011 (NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=152300000 vs ~32-46000000 in adjacent quarters) indicates a significant non-recurring item whose nature is not detailed in the provided data
